comment_content: Hi, I was also expecting a confident victory for Newcastle, because Manchester United didn't have their main players, and I expected a game similar to the one against Everton, maybe not a 1-4 loss, but a 1-3 loss. I don't understand why Newcastle should have taken 3 points from Manchester United, as they were on the bus for most of the match, and Newcastle had plenty of chances. Additionally, the referee didn't give a clear penalty when Manchester United's defender played a handball, which could have been a dog. But I think the next game with Burnley will be a victory for Newcastle, and the home match with Crystal Palace should at least not be lost, and in fact should take 6 points. Newcastle is missing Dan Burn, a key defender. It's an interesting story, and I thought Newcastle would take it, but they played like they did with Sunderland, and Eddie Howe let them down.

comment_content: Sorry, maybe an unpopular opinion but Woltemade isn't good enough and we're cooked if he's our main forward now.  He did the Saint Maximin thing, made an effort in 1 or 2 matches and made some headlines, then week after week of being a total liability.  He's terrible out of possession, always in the wrong position and never where a forward needs to be when service comes in, he loses possession more often than not, and for being the tallest guy on the pitch he doesn't even try to win aerial duels.  His two tap ins last week was his first goals in 10-11 games, the rest of the time he's dragging the team down because he isn't there to capitalise on the effort the rest of the team is putting in.

comment_content:
Atypical performance from Bruno. 
In a game where we had two-thirds of the possession, you’d normally expect him to dominate with dangerous corners, shots from range, and defence-splitting passes. None of that today.

At first I thought he was holding back because of the yellow-card risk, but in the second half he was on the touchline receiving treatment. Looked like a possible muscle issue. If he was carrying a knock, he shouldn’t have stayed on.

We badly needed someone to unlock a compact defence, especially with so little movement in the final third (apart from Hall).
